description: A forestry analyst needs to characterize surface texture patterns in Landsat satellite imagery using GLCM (Grey-Level Co-occurrence Matrix) texture analysis in ESA SNAP. Using the input file ~/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif, perform a GLCM Texture Analysis on band_2 (NIR) with a 7x7 pixel window, computing at minimum the Contrast, Homogeneity, Entropy, and Energy features. Create a new virtual band named exactly 'texture_composite' using the expression (Contrast * Entropy) / (Homogeneity + 1.0), ensuring the expression references the specific GLCM output band names produced. Save the final product containing all GLCM texture bands and the new composite band in BEAM-DIMAP format to ~/snap_exports/landsat_texture.dim and export it as a GeoTIFF to ~/snap_exports/landsat_texture.tif.
